recommendationScope: EX-T2 Tier 1 digital-native only; Tier 2 OCR stack requires separate EX-T3 decision

decisionGateAnswers.DG1_note: liteparse 2.0.7 prebuilt wheels available for Python 3.10-3.14 on Windows/Linux/macOS; no Rust toolchain needed
decisionGateAnswers.DG2_note: LibreOffice not found; required only for LiteParse .docx support; PDF-only use case does not need it
decisionGateAnswers.DG3_note: poppler NOT in PATH on this machine; installable via apt-get (Linux) or third-party binary (Windows)
decisionGateAnswers.DG4_note: EasyOCR downloads ~430MB model weights at first run; LiteParse/Tesseract traineddata files are small and can be pre-staged
decisionGateAnswers.DG5_note: Roadmap rates spatial output LOW now HIGH future; LiteParse provides richer spatial data; Option 1 provides basic bboxes

candidates.name: pdfplumber
candidates.inputFormatFinding: .pdf text layer only; no .docx; no image

candidates.name: pdf2image
candidates.inputFormatFinding: converts .pdf pages to PIL images for OCR; no direct text extraction
candidates.ocrLanguageMappingFinding: N/A - renders images, does not perform OCR
candidates.windowsFeasibilityNote: poppler NOT in PATH; requires manual install and PATH addition
candidates.ciFeasibilityNote: Linux CI: apt-get install poppler-utils; Windows CI: extra manual step

candidates.name: easyocr
candidates.inputFormatFinding: accepts PIL images or numpy arrays; used after pdf2image renders PDF pages
candidates.ocrLanguageMappingFinding: ISO 639-1 codes; en->en; vi->vi; no mapping transformation needed for these two codes
candidates.windowsFeasibilityNote: PyTorch CPU wheel available; large install ~1.2GB
candidates.ciFeasibilityNote: large install + model download at first use; CI must have internet access or pre-cached models

candidates.name: poppler
candidates.inputFormatFinding: provides pdftoppm binary required by pdf2image to rasterize PDF pages
candidates.windowsFeasibilityNote: must manually download third-party binary and add to PATH
candidates.ciFeasibilityNote: Ubuntu CI: apt-get install poppler-utils; Windows CI: extra manual step

candidates.name: liteparse
candidates.license: MIT (Python wrapper) / Apache-2.0 (Rust core per SBOM)
candidates.inputFormatFinding: PDF native via bundled PDFium; .docx/.xlsx/.pptx requires LibreOffice; images require ImageMagick
candidates.ocrLanguageMappingFinding: Tesseract ISO 639-2/T codes: en->eng; vi->vie; mapping layer required; tessdata files must be installed separately
candidates.windowsFeasibility: PASS for PDF; CONDITIONAL for OCR and .docx
candidates.windowsFeasibilityNote: PDF extraction works without extra deps; OCR requires Tesseract install + tessdata; .docx requires LibreOffice
candidates.ciFeasibilityVerdict: PASS for PDF; CONDITIONAL for OCR and .docx
candidates.ciFeasibilityNote: prebuilt wheel no Rust needed; Tesseract install step in CI for OCR; LibreOffice step for .docx
candidates.stackRole: Option 2 unified PDF extractor with bundled PDFium and optional OCR

ocrLanguageMapping.description: Mapping from DSCP DscpDomainProfile.languageCodes to extractor-native OCR codes
ocrLanguageMapping.source: EasyOCR README language table; Tesseract tessdata repository naming convention

claimBoundary: dependency_source_audit_only; no extraction runtime proof; no production readiness; no OCR quality claim; no corpus ingestion; no EC-02 semantic change; no public-sync
